snippets.title: Plain merge
snippets.code:
git switch main
git pull --ff-only                 # safer than plain pull
git merge feature/auth             # fast-forwards if possible, else three-way merge

# Force a merge commit even when fast-forward is possible:
git merge --no-ff feature/auth -m "Land feature/auth"
snippets.explanation: `--no-ff` is policy: many teams want every feature to land as a visible merge commit.

snippets.title: Rebase your feature branch onto latest main
snippets.code:
git switch feature/auth
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main             # replays your commits on top of latest main

# Resolve conflicts as Git stops on each:
# 1. edit conflicted files
# 2. git add <files>
# 3. git rebase --continue

# If you get stuck and want to bail:
git rebase --abort

# Push the rewritten branch:
git push --force-with-lease
snippets.explanation: Rebase resolves conflicts *per commit*, not all at once like a merge. Use `--force-with-lease` to push the rewritten history safely.

snippets.title: Interactive rebase — squash, reorder, reword
snippets.code:
git rebase -i HEAD~5

# Editor opens with:
#
# pick a3f2c9d Add login endpoint
# pick 8b41e07 wip: tests
# pick d1ef000 wip: more tests
# pick 72bd123 fix: typo in error msg
# pick 9aac456 Add rate limit middleware
#
# Change to:
#
# pick a3f2c9d Add login endpoint
# squash 8b41e07 wip: tests
# fixup d1ef000 wip: more tests
# fixup 72bd123 fix: typo in error msg
# pick 9aac456 Add rate limit middleware
#
# Save → opens a second editor for the combined message → save again.
snippets.explanation: `squash` keeps both commit messages for you to merge; `fixup` drops the squashed commit's message entirely.

snippets.title: Cherry-pick a single commit between branches
snippets.code:
git switch hotfix
git cherry-pick a3f2c9d            # apply just that one commit

# If conflicts:
# 1. resolve
# 2. git add <files>
# 3. git cherry-pick --continue
# (or `--abort` to bail)
snippets.explanation: Cherry-pick replays one specific commit somewhere else — useful for porting a fix to a release branch.

snippets.title: Revert a bad commit safely
snippets.code:
git revert <bad_sha>               # creates a NEW commit undoing the bad one

# Revert a merge commit:
git revert -m 1 <merge_sha>        # -m 1 means 'keep the mainline parent'
snippets.explanation: Revert is the safe undo on shared branches — it adds a new commit instead of rewriting history.
